stance_summary: The user is fighting to make a SINGLE direct-quadrature inner product bra^T·(V/Rxy combo)·Ψ̄ reproduce a complex f with a large imaginary part, and treating δ≈0 as a defeat. But their own group's validated reference (COLOSS) never extracts the amplitude as one term: it is f = f_born + f_sc, where the imaginary/scattering content lives in the SPECTRAL f_sc = Σ dn²/(E−Eₙ) and the near-real Born piece is subtracted/added separately. δ≈0 with converged |Mp| is not a failure, it is the signature that the user is computing essentially the Born/real piece and the resolvent (E−H)⁻¹ that carries the phase is absent from the projection. The contrarian moves: (1) stop computing one quadrature and build the spectral resolvent form of Eq.17 from swift's existing eigen(H,B), exactly as COLOSS does in 1-body; (2) decompose the existing Mp into ψ_in×ψ_in / ψ_in×ψ_sc / ψ_sc×ψ_sc to SEE which sub-term should carry the imaginary part — if none does, the operator is wrong, not the prefactor; (3) falsify the Rxy/V vehicle outright by testing the operator identity on the bound state where the answer is known exactly.
anchored_assumption: That the correct realization of Eq.17 in swift is a single direct-quadrature inner product bra^T·(some V·Rxy ordering)·Ψ̄ with a fixed prefactor, and that getting δ≈0 means the prefactor/ordering is slightly wrong (a tuning problem) rather than that the phase-carrying resolvent (E−H)⁻¹ is structurally missing from the projection — i.e. the user is computing a Born-like object and expecting it to contain the full S-matrix phase.

candidates.one_line: Abandon direct quadrature; build the SPECTRAL realization of Eq.17 in swift exactly as COLOSS does — f = f_born + f_sc with f_sc = Σ dₙ²/(E−Eₙ) from swift's existing eigen(H,B), where dₙ = ⟨V-protected bra | eigenvector⟩.
candidates.first_concrete_step: In a new swift/test_eq17_spectral.jl: reuse the SAME (H = T+V·(I+Rxy), B) matrices already assembled by compute_scattering_matrix at θ=10°, call eigen(H, B) to get complex eigenpairs (Eₙ, vₙ) (B-orthonormalize: vₙᵀ B vₘ = δₙₘ via the bilinear c-product, NOT dot), then for the doublet deuteron channel compute dₙ = (Rxy·bra)ᵀ · V · vₙ (the V-protected bra ordering already validated convergent in T4), f_sc = −(e^{iθ}/E_cm)·Σ dₙ²/(E_cm − Eₙ), f_born = −(1/E_cm)·(Rxy·bra)ᵀ·V·ψ_in (Born = source-only, no resolvent), f = f_born + f_sc; print δ,η at nx=12 ny=30. One smoke run.
candidates.info_gain: If it works: confirms the phase was missing because the resolvent (E−H)⁻¹ was never applied — swift was computing a Born-like quadrature, and the group's own spectral form is the right vehicle inside swift. If it fails: tells you the failure is NOT the resolvent but the bra/V operator mapping itself (dₙ wrong), which then isolates the remaining bug to the partial-wave bra construction alone, decoupled from convergence and from the linear solve.
candidates.risk: eigen(H,B) at production size is O(N³) and N=nch·nx·ny can be 10k+; at smoke size (nx=12,ny=30, few channels) it is a few-thousand dense eig, seconds to a minute — fine for the test, but the full spectral sum needs ALL eigenpairs (not just ground state), and complex-symmetric B-orthonormalization of a defective/near-defective spectrum can be numerically delicate. Time loss if the eig spectrum is polluted by spurious complex-rotated continuum states that don't sum cleanly.

orthogonality_to_tried.difference: T1 applies V·Rxy to a SOLVED Ψ̄ in one quadrature; A1 never forms Ψ̄ — it inserts the resolvent (E−H)⁻¹ as a spectral sum over eigen(H,B), so the phase comes from the energy denominators (E−Eₙ), an object T1 has no analog of.
T2 is still a single direct quadrature on ψ_sc; A1 replaces the entire solve-then-project pipeline with the eigendecomposition resolvent — ψ_sc is never computed at all in A1.

T3's −1/E_cm prefactor on a single quadrature is exactly the f_born-only term of A1; A1 ADDS the spectral f_sc on top, which is the piece T3 was missing and which is why T3 could only hit η by coincidence and never δ.
A1 reuses T4's exact V-protected ordering for dₙ and f_born, but Ψ̄ is decomposed spectrally; T4's δ≈0 is predicted by A1 to be the f_born piece alone, with f_sc supplying the missing imaginary part.
A1 keeps bra type fixed; the lever in A1 is the resolvent denominators and the eigen-spectrum, an axis orthogonal to which Riccati combination is used in the bra.

orthogonality_to_tried.tried_item: 关键矛盾 (Rxy^T≠Rxy 674 vs 4.4; δ≈0 = scattering info lost)
orthogonality_to_tried.difference: A1 directly answers '散射信息没进到 Mp' — it injects the scattering information through (E−H)⁻¹ as energy denominators rather than through Rxy ordering, sidestepping the broken Rxy symmetry assumption entirely.

candidates.dependencies: eigen(H,B) on the θ-rotated (H,B) (swift already builds both in compute_scattering_matrix); bilinear c-product B-orthonormalization (the same transpose-not-dot rule already adopted for C_n); COLOSS scatt.f as the line-by-line template (already in tree).

candidates.one_line: Treat δ≈0 as a diagnostic, not a failure: decompose the existing converged Mp into its three sub-terms (ψ_in×ψ_in, ψ_in×ψ_sc, ψ_sc×ψ_sc) and check which carries the imaginary/phase content — if NONE does, the operator is structurally Born-only and the vehicle is falsified.
candidates.first_concrete_step: In test_eq17_green.jl run_case, after forming Ψbar = ψ_total + Rxy·ψ_total with ψ_total = ψ_in + ψ_sc, split it: build Ψbar_in = ψ_in + Rxy·ψ_in and Ψbar_sc = ψ_sc + Rxy·ψ_sc, then compute and print THREE numbers separately: Mp_inin = (Rxy·bra)ᵀ·V·Ψbar_in (bra and ket both from ψ_in), Mp_insc = cross, Mp_scsc = (Rxy·bra)ᵀ·V·Ψbar_sc. Print Re/Im and |·| of each, plus how each scales with mesh. One smoke run.
candidates.info_gain: If Im(Mp_scsc) or Im(Mp_insc) is large and converged but Mp_inin (real, Born-like) dominates the sum: you've localized that the phase is being swamped by a Born term that Eq.17 may not even include for ELASTIC (the diagonal Born often cancels or is absent in the post-form). If every sub-term is near-real: proves the V·Rxy quadrature CANNOT produce a phase regardless of prefactor — falsifies the whole direct-quadrature route in <1 day, which is the highest-value negative result.
candidates.risk: Cheap, low risk; worst case it confirms what is suspected (Born-dominated) without telling you the fix — but even then it converts a vague 'δ≈0' into a precise statement of which term is wrong, which de-risks A1 and A3. Minor risk: the in/sc split of Ψ̄ is not unique under CS back-rotation and a naive split could double-count the source — must subtract the b = V·Rxy·ψ_in source consistently.

orthogonality_to_tried.difference: T1 computes ONE number on the full Ψ̄; A2 dissects that same object into 3 physically-labeled pieces and reads the imaginary part of each — a measurement, not a new ansatz.
T2 used ψ_sc-only as a candidate ANSWER and it jittered; A2 uses the ψ_sc×ψ_sc piece as a DIAGNOSTIC against the other two pieces to see where phase lives, not as the final amplitude.
A2 does not tune any prefactor; it asks which sub-term is non-real before any prefactor is applied, which is exactly the check that would have flagged T3 as a coincidence in advance.
A2 takes T4's converged Mp and explains its δ≈0 by decomposition; it is the immediate follow-up measurement T4 never did.
T5 varied the bra and watched |Mp|; A2 holds bra fixed and varies the KET decomposition (in/sc), an orthogonal axis that T5 never touched.

orthogonality_to_tried.tried_item: 关键矛盾 (δ≈0 = 散射信息没进到 Mp)
orthogonality_to_tried.difference: A2 is the direct experimental test OF that conjecture — it measures the imaginary content of each sub-term to prove or disprove 'scattering info lost', rather than assuming it.

candidates.dependencies: Only the existing test_eq17_green.jl pipeline (ψ_in, ψ_sc, V, Rxy already built); no new physics.

candidates.one_line: Falsify the V·Rxy operator vehicle by testing the operator identity ⟨Ψ|V₂₃+V₃₁|Ψ⟩ = 2⟨Ψ|V₁₂|Ψ⟩ on the KNOWN three-body bound state, building V₂₃ and V₃₁ via separate per-partition transforms (Rxy_31 and Rxy_32 on the correct sides) instead of the symmetric-Rxy shortcut.
candidates.first_concrete_step: In a new swift/test_op_identity.jl: take the converged 3H/deuteron-channel bound state Ψ (from bound2b or the 3-body ground state already available), and compute three numbers at θ=0 first (real, no CS): (a) ⟨Ψ|V|Ψ⟩ using the symmetric Rxy shortcut Mp-style (Rxy·bra)ᵀ·V·Ψ; (b) the explicit V₂₃ = Rxy_32ᵀ·V·Rxy_32 and V₃₁ = Rxy_31ᵀ·V·Rxy_31 built with Rxy_31 and Rxy_32 on their OWN sides; (c) the exact identity value 2⟨Ψ|V₁₂|Ψ⟩ = 2·Ψᵀ·B·V·Ψ-type direct pair expectation. Print all three and their ratios. One smoke run; θ=0 makes everything real and the bound-state expectation values are reference-known.
candidates.info_gain: If (a) and (c) disagree (echoing the 674-vs-4.4 split) but (b) and (c) agree: PROVES the symmetric-Rxy shortcut Rxy=2Rxy_31 and the 'Rxy^T=Rxy' algebra fail in the truncated Laguerre basis, and that V₂₃,V₃₁ must be built with Rxy_31/Rxy_32 on separate sides — directly fixing sub-problem (a) of the stuck card. If even (b) disagrees with (c): the bug is in V or B itself, not the rearrangement — a far deeper and more valuable finding. Either way you get a numerical operator-identity gauge on a state whose answer is known, decoupling the operator question from the entire scattering machinery.
candidates.risk: The 'exact' bound-state identity 2⟨V₁₂⟩ holds for the FULL Ψ but swift uses Faddeev normalization 3⟨Ψ|ψ₃⟩=1 and a truncated space where the identity is only approximate (1-2% truncation already documented); so a few-percent mismatch is expected and you must not over-interpret it. Risk of chasing a truncation artifact as if it were the operator bug — mitigate by checking convergence of the mismatch with mesh.

orthogonality_to_tried.difference: T1 tested the operator on a scattering bra with no known answer; A3 tests the SAME operator class on a bound state with an exact analytic check (2⟨V₁₂⟩), turning an un-anchored divergence into a calibrated identity test.
A3 is entirely at θ=0 on a bound state — no scattering wave, no CS — isolating the rearrangement operator from every continuum/CS complication T2 was entangled in.
A3 has zero free scale: the bound-state expectation value is a known number, so there is nothing to tune — it is the anti-T3 (a true reference check, the kind the budget demands).
A3 directly stress-tests the algebraic step T4 RELIED ON (⟨bra|V₂₃+V₃₁|Ψ̄⟩=(Rxy·bra)ᵀV Ψ̄ via Rxy symmetry) on a case where its correctness can be verified, rather than assuming it and finding δ≈0.
A3 does not involve the asymptotic bra at all; it probes the interior operator algebra, an axis T5 never examined.
A3 is the controlled experiment that turns the 674-vs-4.4 mystery into a quantitative, reference-anchored statement of how badly Rxy=2Rxy_31 breaks and whether separate-side V₂₃/V₃₁ repairs it.

candidates.dependencies: Access to a converged 3-body bound-state Ψ in the deuteron channels (bound2b gives the 2-body deuteron; for a genuine 3-body identity test, the 3H ground state from the bound solver is preferable); Rxy_31 and Rxy_32 available separately (Rxy_matrix_optimized returns Rxy_31; Rxy_32=Rxy_31ᵀ per code — A3 also tests whether that transpose claim itself holds).

candidates.one_line: Cross-check swift's elastic amplitude against an INDEPENDENT extractor that needs no asymptotic projection and no Rxy operator mapping: the two Kohn-variational integral relations (Barletta/Garrido), which give tan δ as a ratio of two interior matrix elements of the SAME ψ_sc swift already solves.
candidates.first_concrete_step: In a new swift/test_kohn_integral_relations.jl: reuse the already-solved ψ_sc and ψ_in (no new solve). Form the two Kohn integral relations for the doublet channel — schematically I_S = ⟨S-source | V | Ψ⟩ and I_C = ⟨C-source | V | Ψ⟩ with a regular (F-type) and irregular (G-type) free-source pair built from the SAME compute_initial_state_vector machinery — and compute tan δ = I_S / (something with I_C) per Garrido2012 Eq. for n+d. Print δ, compare to 105.49°. One smoke run reusing cached ψ_sc.
candidates.info_gain: If Kohn gives ~105° from the SAME ψ_sc that Eq.17 gives δ≈0 from: proves ψ_sc is physically correct and the bug is purely in the Eq.17 PROJECTION operator (Rxy/V mapping), not in the scattering solve — a decisive localization. If Kohn ALSO gives δ≈0: proves ψ_sc itself is missing the scattering content (the linear solve or the source b is wrong), which would redirect the whole effort upstream of amplitude extraction. Either result cleanly partitions blame between solve and extract.
candidates.risk: This is a logged, n-d-validated method in the user's wiki (integral-relations / Garrido2012), so the cross-checker may flag it as 'known to user' — but the user picked Eq.17 (path B) and has NOT used it as an independent CROSS-CHECK of swift's ψ_sc; the proposal here is strictly diagnostic (validate ψ_sc), not 'switch to Kohn as the production extractor', which keeps it in-bounds. Real numerical risk: the Kohn ratio is sensitive to the choice of the two free comparison functions and to the same C_n normalization subtlety; getting the relation's exact n+d partial-wave form right from Garrido is non-trivial and could eat the day.

orthogonality_to_tried.difference: T1 uses Rxy·V on Ψ̄ to project; A4 uses NO Rxy at all — the Kohn relations act on ψ_sc directly through V with free regular/irregular comparison sources, a different functional entirely.

T2 was a single ad-hoc quadrature on ψ_sc; A4 is the variationally-correct RATIO of two specific integral relations whose value is stationary and benchmark-validated for n+d — a principled extractor, not a guess.
Kohn's tan δ is a RATIO, so the overall scale and prefactor cancel — A4 is structurally immune to the scale-coincidence trap that invalidated T3.
A4 removes the Rxy operator from the extraction completely, so it is independent of the broken Rxy^T=Rxy assumption that A3 probes and that T4 silently depends on.

A4 uses BOTH a regular and an irregular comparison source by design (in a ratio), rather than scanning a single bra type to see which |Mp| wins — the irregular-G dominance that confused T5 is here a controlled, intended ingredient.

orthogonality_to_tried.tried_item: 关键矛盾 (δ≈0 = 散射信息没进到投影)
orthogonality_to_tried.difference: A4 is the cleanest test of whether the scattering info is in ψ_sc at all (vs lost in the projection): if Kohn recovers δ from ψ_sc, the info was there and only the Eq.17 vehicle lost it.

candidates.dependencies: The already-solved ψ_sc and ψ_in (cached, no new solve); Garrido2012 / Barletta2009 partial-wave integral-relation formulae for n+d (in the user's wiki [[methods/integral-relations]]); regular+irregular free comparison sources from compute_initial_state_vector (F already there; G/irregular needs the gc branch, already accessible via coulcc).

convergence_note: A1 (spectral COLOSS-style resolvent inside swift) is the most likely overlap with the methodologist solver, since it is the cheapest-validated route the user's own group already proved works and it reuses existing eigen(H,B) machinery. A2 (sub-term decomposition diagnostic) is also a plausible convergence pick as the lowest-cost next measurement. A3 (bound-state operator-identity falsification) and A4 (Kohn cross-check of ψ_sc) are the more contrarian, vehicle-falsifying moves less likely to be independently proposed.
